name: Fruit Souffle Omelet
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup orange juice
1/3 cup mixed dried fruit cut into strips, (prunes, apples, apricots and pears)
recipeInstructions:
The sauce: In a small saucepan, bring the orange juice and water to a boil. Remove from heat, stir in the cornstarch slurry; return to heat and bring to a boil for 1 minute to thicken and clear. Remove from heat and let cool. Put the yoghurt in a small bowl and gently whisk into the warm orange mixture.
The filling: Combine the mixed dried fruit and cranberries and set aside.
The omelet: In a small bowl, combine the egg substitute, egg yolks, arrowroot, and 1/2 teaspoon of cardamom. In another bowl, beat the egg whites with cream of tartar and brown sugar until they form soft peaks. Just before cooking, fold the egg yolk mixture into the egg whites until even pale yellow.
Pre-heat the broiler and set the rack 4-5 inches (10 to 13 cm) from the heating element. Set the skillet over medium heat and, when hot, add butter, letting it sizzle until light brown. Add the omelet mixture all at once and whisk for about 10 seconds, counting slowly. Sprinkle dried fruit filling over the top, pressing down with a fork to get beneath the surface.
Cook until the edges of the omelet come away from the pan, air holes appear on the top, and you can smell brown sugar starting to caramelize (about 2 minutes). Immediately slide the pan under the pre-heated broiler and cook until browned on top and risen (about 4 minutes). Remove from oven, loosen edges with a spatula, and carefully shake omelet out of pan onto cutting board.
To serve: Cut into 4 wedges, place on individual serving plates, spoon sauce over top, garnish with cranberries and almonds, and sprinkle remaining ground cardamom.
